ralph-loop.template: You are entering a Ralph Loop. Follow the ralph-loop skill (auto-loaded from ~/.claude/skills/ralph-loop/). Parse the user's arguments: first parts are the task description, --max-iterations N (integer), --completion-promise "TEXT". Initialize .opencode/ralph-loop.state.md and begin iteration 1.
ralph-loop.description: Start a Ralph loop: iterative self-correction loop until task completion

cancel-ralph.template: The user wants to cancel the active Ralph loop. Remove .opencode/ralph-loop.state.md if it exists. Report cancellation to the user.

tauri:dev.template: Launch Tauri dev with WebView2 CDP remote debugging. Run `pnpm tauri:dev:cdp` from the project root in a background process (Start-Process). Wait 15 seconds for the dev server to initialize, then run `pnpm cdp:smoke` to verify the CDP endpoint at http://127.0.0.1:9222 is reachable. Report the result.
tauri:dev.description: Launch Tauri dev with WebView2 CDP remote debugging (port 9222)

cdp:smoke.template: Run `pnpm cdp:smoke` from the project root to probe the WebView2 CDP endpoint at http://127.0.0.1:9222. Report the version, protocol, WebSocket endpoint, and target list. If the Ridge page target is found, highlight it.
cdp:smoke.description: Probe WebView2 CDP endpoint and list debuggable targets

cdp:target.template: List the debuggable targets from the WebView2 CDP endpoint at http://127.0.0.1:9222/json/list. Report the type, title, and URL of each target. Use the chrome-devtools MCP server tools to interact with the Ridge webview if it's running.
cdp:target.description: List debuggable targets from WebView2 CDP endpoint
